title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: Pure-R parser .Q-View multiplex ELISA project files.

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: qviewparsR pure R – compiled code, Java runtime, H2 database driver, system libraries – installs identically Windows, macOS, Linux. hard prerequisite R >= 4.1.0. CRAN (released): Development version GitHub: Platform notes: Windows Rtools required (package compiles nothing CRAN dependencies arrive binaries); macOS nothing beyond R needed; Linux package pure R, dependencies (dplyr, readr, tidyr, openxlsx2) contain C++ build source unless use binary repository (example Posit Public Package Manager r2u), avoids needing compiler. package depends small tidyverse-aligned core (cli, dplyr, lifecycle, openxlsx2, readr, rlang, tibble, tidyr). Plotting requires ggplot2; publication overview adds patchwork; Shiny front-end adds shiny, bslib, DT, withr.
code: install.packages("qviewparsR") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("CTTIR/qviewparsR")

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: read_qview() always returns list class qview eleven slots described ?read_qview. Empty slots zero-row tibbles rather NULL, downstream code can rely shape stability.
code: library(qviewparsR)  qv <- read_qview("path/to/plate.Q-View") qv                          # one-screen summary  qv$analytes                 # spot_number, analyte, unit, lod, lloq, uloq qv$well_groups              # one row per sample / calibrator / control qv$pixel_intensities        # long-format replicate readings qv$summary_statistics       # per-group mean / std-dev / CV rows qv$plate_layout             # one row per plate well  summary(qv)                 # mean / SD / CV per well type x analyte

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: producing software rewrites identifiers original well-assignment template storing . mapping systematic reversible: strip_qview_prefix() reverses rewrite. Pass strip_prefix = TRUE read_qview() apply across every sample-id column :
code: qv <- read_qview("path/to/plate.Q-View", strip_prefix = TRUE) unique(qv$well_groups$sample_id)  strip_qview_prefix(c("ICal 1", "GLow", "HHigh", "NFD24277364")) #> [1] "Cal 1"      "Low"        "High"       "FD24277364"

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: as_tibble() returns long-format pixel_intensities table – primary tabular payload – parsed object drops straight dplyr / ggplot2 pipeline:
code: library(dplyr)  qv |>   as_tibble() |>   filter(replicate == 1L) |>   group_by(analyte, unit) |>   summarise(median_pi = median(pixel_intensity, na.rm = TRUE),             .groups = "drop")

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: plot.qview() method offers three quick-look views, coloured viridis ramp Shiny app uses: call returns ggplot object, themes, scales, labels can added top:
code: plot(qv, type = "plate_map")          # 96-well plate, fill = well type plot(qv, type = "intensity_heatmap")  # facet per analyte, fill = PI plot(qv, type = "replicate_scatter")  # rep 1 vs rep 2 per analyte library(ggplot2) plot(qv, type = "plate_map") +   theme_bw(base_size = 12) +   labs(title = NULL, subtitle = "QC overview")

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: Three writers cover common destinations. return parsed object invisibly, compose |>: write_qview_xlsx() write_qview_rds() accept overwrite = TRUE replace existing destinations. legacy aliases qview_to_xlsx() qview_to_csv_dir() still work emit lifecycle::deprecate_warn(); switch new names.
code: qv |>   write_qview_xlsx("plate.xlsx") |>      # one sheet per parsed table   write_qview_csv ("plate_csv/") |>      # one CSV per parsed table   write_qview_rds ("plate.rds")          # full lossless R round-trip

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: Every well assignment already embedded .Q-View file. also original well-assignment template CSV producing application imported, read_qview_template() parses tibble aligns qv$plate_layout: rows surviving filter expose template-vs-Q-View mismatches.
code: tmpl <- read_qview_template("path/to/template.csv")  qv$plate_layout |>   dplyr::left_join(tmpl, by = "well", suffix = c("_qview", "_template")) |>   dplyr::filter(sample_id_qview != sample_id_template)

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: non-coding collaborators, qview_app() launches Shiny app monochrome aesthetic (light + dark mode) hex sticker upper-left corner: app accepts .Q-View upload (optionally template CSV) exposes: publication-ready 2x2 Overview figure (plate layout / pixel intensity distribution / replicate concordance / mean PI well type) high-DPI PNG vector PDF export; every parsed table xlsx download; one-click xlsx / rds / csv-zip whole project; built-dark/light toggle per-card max/restore.
code: qview_app()             # 512 MB upload cap by default qview_app(max_upload_mb = 1024)

title: Reading and analysing .Q-View files with qviewparsR
text: Every exported function validates inputs early raises structured cli::cli_abort() error pointing user’s call rather internal helpers, e.g.: bullet always carries actionable hint one exists.
code: Error in `read_qview()`: ! `path` must be an existing file. x "missing.Q-View" does not exist. Error in `read_qview()`: ! `path` is not a valid `.Q-View` project file. x "junk.bin" is missing the expected container header. i Expected a numeric container version followed by "Q-View Project".

title: qviewparsR
text: qviewparsR pure-R parser binary .Q-View project file format used chemiluminescent multiplex ELISA plate imaging quantification. extracts embedded report returns tidy tibbles. Java runtime, H2 database driver, system dependencies beyond working R installation.

title: qviewparsR
text: qviewparsR pure R — compiled code, Java runtime, H2 database driver, system libraries — installs way Windows, macOS, Linux. hard prerequisite R >= 4.1.0. CRAN (released) get ready--use binary: development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("qviewparsR") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("CTTIR/qviewparsR")

title: qviewparsR
text: Windows — Rtools required: qviewparsR compiles nothing, CRAN dependencies install pre-built binaries. macOS — nothing beyond R (XQuartz needed). Linux — package pure R, dependencies (dplyr, readr, tidyr, openxlsx2) contain C++ build source unless use binary repository Posit Public Package Manager r2u — avoids needing compiler. Otherwise install build toolchain (e.g. build-essential Debian/Ubuntu). Installing package pulls required imports automatically. optional features need extra packages: plotting uses ggplot2 (plus patchwork overview figure), interactive app (qview_app()) uses shiny, bslib, DT.

title: qviewparsR
code: library(qviewparsR)  qv <- read_qview("path/to/project.Q-View") qv                                # compact summary qv$analytes                       # spot_number, analyte, unit, lod, lloq, ... qv$pixel_intensities              # long-format replicate readings qv$plate_layout                   # one row per well  plot(qv, type = "plate_map")      # plate visualisation (needs ggplot2) plot(qv, type = "intensity_heatmap") plot(qv, type = "replicate_scatter")  # Export (pipe-friendly: each writer returns qv invisibly) qv |>   write_qview_xlsx("out.xlsx") |>   write_qview_csv("out_csv/")  |>   write_qview_rds("out.rds")  # Per-analyte mean / SD / CV per well-type group summary(qv)  # Interactive front-end (upload, visualise, download) qview_app()

title: qviewparsR
text: .Q-View file single-file container bundles embedded H2 SQL database (Java, version 0.5/B) binary LOB files holding chemiluminescent plate images. file ZIP archive, XML, CSV — proprietary binary container plain-text manifest header followed concatenated H2 segments.

title: qviewparsR
text: H2 segment starts -- H2 0.5/B -- triplet marker. database uses 2048-byte pages.
code: +-----------------------------------------+ | Bytes 0 - ~290:  text manifest header   | |   - container version                   | |   - declared file entries (size + name) | +-----------------------------------------+ | Segment 1: main H2 SQL database         | |   - 36 tables (see schema below)        | |   - the rendered CSV report (CLOB)      | +-----------------------------------------+ | Segment 2: LOB file 1 (image data)      | +-----------------------------------------+ | Segment 3: LOB file 2 (more LOB data)   | +-----------------------------------------+

title: qviewparsR
text: qviewparsR recovers data embedded CSV report; table diagram documents underlying schema reference.

text: Q-View imports well-assignment template CSV prefixes identifiers internally: strip_qview_prefix() reverses transformation, read_qview(path, strip_prefix = TRUE) applies across whole returned object.

code: path <- system.file("extdata", "example.Q-View", package = "qviewparsR") qv <- read_qview(path, verbose = FALSE) tibble::as_tibble(qv) #> # A tibble: 20 × 8 #>    well_group sample_id well  replicate analyte unit  pixel_intensity dilution #>    <chr>      <chr>     <chr>     <int> <chr>   <chr>           <dbl>    <dbl> #>  1 ICal 1     ICal 1    A1            1 Ba      ng/ml            8671       NA #>  2 ICal 1     ICal 1    A1            1 Bb      ug/ml           19982       NA #>  3 ICal 1     ICal 1    A2            2 Ba      ng/ml           17838       NA #>  4 ICal 1     ICal 1    A2            2 Bb      ug/ml           23848       NA #>  5 N12345     N12345    A3            1 Ba      ng/ml            1200       NA #>  6 N12345     N12345    A3            1 Bb      ug/ml            1300       NA #>  7 N12345     N12345    A4            2 Ba      ng/ml            1250       NA #>  8 N12345     N12345    A4            2 Bb      ug/ml            1350       NA #>  9 ICal 2     ICal 2    B1            1 Ba      ng/ml            4000       NA #> 10 ICal 2     ICal 2    B1            1 Bb      ug/ml            5000       NA #> 11 ICal 2     ICal 2    B2            2 Ba      ng/ml            4100       NA #> 12 ICal 2     ICal 2    B2            2 Bb      ug/ml            5100       NA #> 13 GLow       GLow      G1            1 Ba      ng/ml             300       NA #> 14 GLow       GLow      G1            1 Bb      ug/ml             400       NA #> 15 GLow       GLow      G2            2 Ba      ng/ml             310       NA #> 16 GLow       GLow      G2            2 Bb      ug/ml             410       NA #> 17 HHigh      HHigh     H1            1 Ba      ng/ml           12000       NA #> 18 HHigh      HHigh     H1            1 Bb      ug/ml           15000       NA #> 19 HHigh      HHigh     H2            2 Ba      ng/ml           12100       NA #> 20 HHigh      HHigh     H2            2 Bb      ug/ml           15100       NA

title: Plot a Q-View object — plot.qview
text: "plate_map" – heat-coloured plate map, one cell per well, fill well type (standard / negative / sample / control). "intensity_heatmap" – per-analyte facet, fill replicate-1 pixel intensity per well. "replicate_scatter" – replicate 1 vs replicate 2 pixel intensity per analyte. Requires ggplot2 package (Suggested).

title: Plot a Q-View object — plot.qview
code: path <- system.file("extdata", "example.Q-View", package = "qviewparsR") qv <- read_qview(path, verbose = FALSE) plot(qv, type = "plate_map")  plot(qv, type = "replicate_scatter")

title: Launch the qviewparsR Q-View Shiny app — qview_app
text: Interactive front-end read_qview(). Uploads .Q-View file (optionally accompanying well-assignment template CSV), displays parsed metadata, analytes, well groups, replicate tables, lets user download parsed result xlsx, rds, zip per-table CSV files. Requires shiny, bslib, DT packages (listed Suggests).

title: Launch the qviewparsR Q-View Shiny app — qview_app
code: if (interactive()) {   qview_app() }

what: qviewparsR: Read .Q-View Multiplex ELISA Project Files — qviewparsR-package
title: qviewparsR: Read .Q-View Multiplex ELISA Project Files — qviewparsR-package
text: Pure-R parser .Q-View binary project files used chemiluminescent multiplex ELISA plate imaging quantification. Extracts pixel intensities, analyte mappings, sample assignments, plate layout, optional curve-fit parameters, embedded CSV report H2 database container format. Returns tidy tibbles ready downstream statistical analysis.

title: qviewparsR: Read .Q-View Multiplex ELISA Project Files — qviewparsR-package
text: Reader: read_qview() – parse .Q-View file. Helpers: strip_qview_prefix() reverse Q-View internal naming convention; well_label() map (row, column) plate notation. Optional: read_qview_template() parse Q-View well-assignment template CSV. Methods: is_qview(), print.qview(), plot.qview(), as_tibble.qview(). Shiny: qview_app() interactive upload / preview / download.

title: qviewparsR: Read .Q-View Multiplex ELISA Project Files — qviewparsR-package
text: Maintainer: Raban Heller raban.heller@charite.de (ORCID) [copyright holder] Authors: Raban Heller raban.heller@charite.de (ORCID) [copyright holder] Marco Mannes

title: Read a .Q-View project file — read_qview
code: read_qview(   path,   strip_prefix = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   call = rlang::caller_env() )

title: Read a .Q-View project file — read_qview
text: path Character. Path .Q-View file. strip_prefix Logical. TRUE, reverse Q-View's internal naming convention via strip_qview_prefix() identifiers match original well-assignment template. Default FALSE. verbose Logical. Print short summary parsing. Default TRUE. call execution environment calling function. Used error reporting; experts .

title: Read a .Q-View project file — read_qview
text: list class "qview" containing: metadata Named list: project, plate, image, imager, product, user, report_created, qview_version, template, container_version, file_path, parsed_at. manifest Tibble one row per declared file entry (name, size_bytes, parent). segments Tibble H2 segment byte ranges (segment, start, end, size). analytes Tibble: spot_number, analyte, unit, plus lod, lloq, uloq, assay_control_low, assay_control_high reported. well_groups Tibble: well_group, sample_id, is_standard, is_negative, is_sample, is_control, well_type. pixel_intensities Long-format tibble replicate readings: well_group, sample_id, well, replicate, analyte, unit, pixel_intensity, dilution. summary_statistics Long-format tibble per-group averages, std-dev, CV statistics: well_group, sample_id, statistic, analyte, value, unit. concentrations Long-format concentration tibble NULL regression model "Qualitative". curve_fit Tibble analyte, regression_model, NULL reported. report_csv Character vector raw CSV report lines, NULL report generated. plate_layout Tibble one row per well: well, plate_row, plate_col, well_group, sample_id, well_type, dilution.

title: Read a .Q-View project file — read_qview
text: Parses .Q-View binary container (chemiluminescent multiplex ELISA project file holding embedded H2 database plus binary LOB segments) extracts assay data: project metadata, analyte panel units, well-group sample assignments, per-well replicate pixel intensities, summary statistics, (present) embedded CSV report. file format reverse-engineered public binary inspection: begins plain-text manifest, followed three concatenated H2 database segments. fully-formatted report Q-View renders user stored CLOB inside main H2 segment. parser scans binary CLOB, reassembles across H2 page boundaries (2048-byte pages), parses CSV. Parsing done pure R: Java runtime, H2 database driver, system dependencies beyond working R installation.

title: Read a .Q-View project file — read_qview
code: # A small synthetic .Q-View ships with the package: path <- system.file("extdata", "example.Q-View", package = "qviewparsR") qv <- read_qview(path) #> ✔ Parsed example.Q-View: 5 well groups x 3 analytes (20 replicate rows). #> ℹ Q-View Version: "3.13" qv #>  #> ── Q-View project: "Example ELISA project" ───────────────────────────────────── #> • Plate: "Plate 1" #> • Image: "example-plate (01 Jan 2024 12:00)" #> • Imager: "#000000 (00000)" #> • Product: "EXAMPLE-LOT" #> • Software: v"3.13" #> • Template: "example-template" #> • Created: "01 Jan 2024 12:05" #>  #> ── Analytes (3) ── #>  #> Ba (ng/ml), Bb (ug/ml), Ref Spot (N/A) #>  #> ── Well groups (5) ── #>  #> • standard: 2 #> • negative: 1 #> • control: 1 #> • sample: 1 #>  #> ── Data ── #>  #> • replicate rows: 20 #> • summary stat rows: 0 #> • concentrations: 4 rows #> • curve fit: 3 analytes qv$analytes #> # A tibble: 3 × 8 #>   spot_number analyte  unit       lod    lloq  uloq assay_control_low #>         <int> <chr>    <chr>    <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>             <dbl> #> 1           1 Ba       ng/ml  0.065    0.26    18.9                NA #> 2           2 Bb       ug/ml  0.00051  0.0029   0.2                NA #> 3           3 Ref Spot N/A   NA       NA       NA                5000 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: assay_control_high <dbl> head(qv$pixel_intensities) #> # A tibble: 6 × 8 #>   well_group sample_id well  replicate analyte unit  pixel_intensity dilution #>   <chr>      <chr>     <chr>     <int> <chr>   <chr>           <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 ICal 1     ICal 1    A1            1 Ba      ng/ml            8671       NA #> 2 ICal 1     ICal 1    A1            1 Bb      ug/ml           19982       NA #> 3 ICal 1     ICal 1    A2            2 Ba      ng/ml           17838       NA #> 4 ICal 1     ICal 1    A2            2 Bb      ug/ml           23848       NA #> 5 N12345     N12345    A3            1 Ba      ng/ml            1200       NA #> 6 N12345     N12345    A3            1 Bb      ug/ml            1300       NA  if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   plot(qv, type = "plate_map") }

title: Read a Q-View well-assignment template CSV — read_qview_template
code: read_qview_template(path, verbose = TRUE, call = rlang::caller_env())

title: Read a Q-View well-assignment template CSV — read_qview_template
text: path Path template file (csv). verbose Logical. Print short summary parsing. Default TRUE. call execution environment calling function. Used error reporting; experts .

title: Read a Q-View well-assignment template CSV — read_qview_template
text: tibble::tibble() one row per well columns well (character, e.g. "A1"), plate_row (character, ""..), plate_col (integer), sample_id (character), group_type (character), dilution (numeric).

title: Read a Q-View well-assignment template CSV — read_qview_template
text: Parses plate-template CSV Q-View imports sample assignment. file uses multi-section layout: NxM cell top-left declares plate dimensions, followed sections labelled Group Name, Group Type, Dilution Factor. section one row per plate row one column per plate column. template data also embedded inside .Q-View file (recovered read_qview()); function exists setting new plates cross-validating Q-View imports original template.

title: Read a Q-View well-assignment template CSV — read_qview_template
code: path <- system.file("extdata", "example-template.csv",                     package = "qviewparsR") layout <- read_qview_template(path) #> ✔ Parsed Q-View template: 96 wells, 19 unique samples from #>   /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/qviewparsR/extdata/example-template.csv. head(layout) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   well  plate_row plate_col sample_id group_type dilution #>   <chr> <chr>         <int> <chr>     <chr>         <dbl> #> 1 A1    A                 1 Cal 1     calibrator       NA #> 2 B1    B                 1 Cal 2     calibrator       NA #> 3 C1    C                 1 Cal 3     calibrator       NA #> 4 D1    D                 1 Cal 4     calibrator       NA #> 5 E1    E                 1 Cal 5     calibrator       NA #> 6 F1    F                 1 Cal 6     calibrator       NA

title: Reverse the Q-View internal naming convention — strip_qview_prefix
code: strip_qview_prefix(x)

title: Reverse the Q-View internal naming convention — strip_qview_prefix
text: x Character vector Q-View internal names.

title: Reverse the Q-View internal naming convention — strip_qview_prefix
text: Character vector length x, prefixes stripped. NAs unrecognised values pass unchanged.

title: Reverse the Q-View internal naming convention — strip_qview_prefix
text: import, producing software prefixes well-assignment template names single-letter codes: "Cal 1" ... "Cal N" -> "ICal 1" ... "ICal N" (Internal calibrator) "Low" -> "GLow" "High" -> "HHigh" "FD..." -digit ID -> "N..." (sample prefix) strip_qview_prefix() reverses transformation downstream code sees identifiers exactly appear original template CSV. Strings match known prefix pattern returned unchanged.

title: Reverse the Q-View internal naming convention — strip_qview_prefix

title: Summary statistics for a Q-View object — summary.qview
text: tibble::tibble() one row per well_type x analyte combination, columns: well_type, analyte, unit, n, mean, sd, cv, min, max.

title: Summary statistics for a Q-View object — summary.qview
code: path <- system.file("extdata", "example.Q-View", package = "qviewparsR") qv <- read_qview(path, verbose = FALSE) summary(qv) #>  #> ── Q-View summary ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Mean / SD / CV of pixel intensities, grouped by well type: #> # A tibble: 8 × 9 #>   well_type analyte unit      n   mean      sd      cv   min   max #>   <fct>     <chr>   <chr> <int>  <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 standard  Ba      ng/ml     4  8652. 6500.   0.751    4000 17838 #> 2 standard  Bb      ug/ml     4 13482. 9864.   0.732    5000 23848 #> 3 negative  Ba      ng/ml     2   305     7.07 0.0232    300   310 #> 4 negative  Bb      ug/ml     2   405     7.07 0.0175    400   410 #> 5 sample    Ba      ng/ml     2  1225    35.4  0.0289   1200  1250 #> 6 sample    Bb      ug/ml     2  1325    35.4  0.0267   1300  1350 #> 7 control   Ba      ng/ml     2 12050    70.7  0.00587 12000 12100 #> 8 control   Bb      ug/ml     2 15050    70.7  0.00470 15000 15100

title: Convert plate row / column to a well label — well_label
code: well_label(row, col, zero_based = FALSE)

title: Convert plate row / column to a well label — well_label
text: row Integer character. Either 0/1-based row index already single letter (""...). col Integer column index (1-based 0-based; values >= 1 treated 1-based). zero_based Logical. TRUE, treat row col 0-based; FALSE (default), treat 1-based.

title: Convert plate row / column to a well label — well_label
text: Character vector well labels (e.g. "A1", "H12"), recycled longer row / col.

title: Convert plate row / column to a well label — well_label
text: Converts 0-based 1-based row column indices standard "A1" ... "H12" plate notation. Vectorised.

title: Convert plate row / column to a well label — well_label
code: well_label(0, 0, zero_based = TRUE)   # "A1" #> [1] "A1" well_label(7, 11, zero_based = TRUE)  # "H12" #> [1] "H12" well_label("C", 5)                    # "C5" #> [1] "C5"

title: Write a Q-View object to disk — write_qview
code: write_qview_xlsx(   qv,   path,   template = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   call = rlang::caller_env() )  write_qview_csv(qv, path, template = NULL, call = rlang::caller_env())  write_qview_rds(qv, path, overwrite = FALSE, call = rlang::caller_env())  qview_to_xlsx(   qv,   path,   template = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   call = rlang::caller_env() )  qview_to_csv_dir(qv, dir, template = NULL, call = rlang::caller_env())

title: Write a Q-View object to disk — write_qview
text: qv qview object read_qview(). path Output path. write_qview_xlsx() / write_qview_rds() single file path; write_qview_csv() output directory (created exist). template Optional plate-template tibble (read_qview_template()) include extra sheet / file. overwrite Logical. FALSE (default), existing destination triggers error; set TRUE replace . Ignored write_qview_csv() (adds files). call execution environment calling function. Used error reporting; experts . dir Deprecated alias path accepted qview_to_csv_dir(). Use path instead.

title: Write a Q-View object to disk — write_qview
text: qv, invisibly, support pipelines.

title: Write a Q-View object to disk — write_qview
text: Three writers, one common destination. return input qv invisibly compose pipe. write_qview_xlsx() – one sheet per parsed table. write_qview_csv()  – one CSV per parsed table inside directory. write_qview_rds()  – single .rds containing full qview object (lossless, round-trippable format).

title: Write a Q-View object to disk — write_qview
code: path <- system.file("extdata", "example.Q-View", package = "qviewparsR") qv <- read_qview(path, verbose = FALSE)  out <- tempfile() dir.create(out) qv |>   write_qview_rds(file.path(out, "plate.rds")) |>   write_qview_csv(file.path(out, "plate_csv")) list.files(out, recursive = TRUE) #> [1] "plate.rds"                       "plate_csv/analytes.csv"          #> [3] "plate_csv/concentrations.csv"    "plate_csv/curve_fit.csv"         #> [5] "plate_csv/manifest.csv"          "plate_csv/metadata.csv"          #> [7] "plate_csv/pixel_intensities.csv" "plate_csv/plate_layout.csv"      #> [9] "plate_csv/well_groups.csv"

title: qviewparsR 1.0.0
text: Initial release. Pure-R parser .Q-View binary project files (chemiluminescent multiplex ELISA plate imaging quantification). Java runtime, H2 database driver, compiled code.

title: qviewparsR 1.0.0
text: read_qview() now resolves superseded MVCC page versions embedded H2 container retains. version truncated 2048-byte page boundary (fewer analytes, number cut mid-digit) longer mistaken current reading: physical (well, replicate, analyte) value occurring often across committed page copies wins, breaking ties toward complete source row. Per-well pixel intensities now match Q-View’s grid exports 1e-6, well appears plate_layout. read_qview_template() auto-detects field separator, semicolon-delimited (European-locale) tab-delimited templates parse comma-delimited ones. plot(type = "replicate_scatter") longer errors well-group label maps one well; duplicate readings averaged.

title: qviewparsR 1.0.0
text: read_qview(path, strip_prefix = FALSE): parses .Q-View container returns list class qview project metadata, analyte panel (units, LOD / LLOQ / ULOQ, assay-control range), well-group sample assignments, per-well replicate pixel intensities, summary statistics, optional back-calculated concentrations, curve fits, plate layout (tidy tibbles). read_qview_template(): parses companion well-assignment template CSV (NxM layout Group Name / Group Type / Dilution Factor sections).

title: qviewparsR 1.0.0
text: strip_qview_prefix(): reverses producer-side naming convention (ICal N -> Cal N, GLow -> Low, HHigh -> High, NFD... / N1234... -> original sample ID). well_label(): vectorised plate-coordinate helper.

title: qviewparsR 1.0.0
text: is_qview(): predicate S3 class. print.qview(): compact one-screen summary. summary.qview(): per-analyte mean / SD / CV / min / max grouped well type, returned qview_summary tibble print method. plot.qview(type = ...): quick-look plate map, per-analyte intensity heatmap, replicate-1-vs-2 scatter; viridis throughout. as_tibble.qview(): long-format pixel-intensity tibble.

title: qviewparsR 1.0.0
text: write_qview_xlsx(), write_qview_csv(), write_qview_rds(): pipe-friendly writers return parsed object invisibly. qview_to_xlsx() / qview_to_csv_dir() kept lifecycle::deprecate_warn() aliases back-compatibility.

title: qviewparsR 1.0.0
text: qview_app(): monochrome bslib Shiny app built-dark/light toggle, hex-sticker brand, large upload cap (default 512 MB), per-table xlsx download, publication-ready 2x2 Overview tab (plate layout / pixel-intensity distribution / replicate concordance / mean PI well type) high-DPI PNG vector PDF export.
